summary: Completed comprehensive frontend testing of Tie-Down Calculator feature at /calculator route. All math calculations verified correct per 49 CFR 393.100-393.136 regulations. All UI components working properly including preset buttons, custom WLL input, direct/indirect toggle, defective toggle, reset button, back navigation, and reference section toggle. Mobile responsive design verified.
passed_tests: Calculator page loads at /calculator route
Cargo weight input accepts numeric values
Cargo length input accepts numeric values
Required WLL calculation: 50% of cargo weight (§393.104) - verified with 30000 lbs = 15000 WLL
Minimum tie-down count: 1 for <5ft & <1100lbs - verified with 1000 lbs, 4ft = 1 min
Minimum tie-down count: 2 for 5-10ft - verified with 5ft and 10ft
Minimum tie-down count: 2+ceil((length-10)/10) for >10ft - verified with 11ft=3, 20ft=3, 21ft=4, 30ft=4, 31ft=5
Quick-add preset buttons: 1" Ratchet Strap (3300 WLL), 2" Ratchet Strap (3300 WLL), 4" Ratchet Strap (5400 WLL), 3/8" Gr70 Chain (6600 WLL), 1/2" Gr70 Chain (11300 WLL)
Custom tie-down with manually entered WLL - verified 5000 lbs custom entry
Direct method: 100% WLL contribution - verified 6600 lbs = 6600 lbs eff.
Indirect method: 50% WLL contribution - verified 11300 lbs = 5650 lbs eff. with 50% indicator
Defective toggle: 0 lbs effective WLL - verified with DEFECTIVE — 0 lbs display
Circular SVG compliance gauge: shows correct percentage (66%, 155%, 133%, 58%, 44%, 660%)
WLL progress bar fills correctly with color coding (red <60%, amber 60-99%, green >=100%)
Count dots: red X for defective, green checkmark for active, empty dashed for still-needed
Compliance status: COMPLIANT when WLL >= required AND count >= min
Compliance status: NOT COMPLIANT with correct message (Insufficient WLL, Need X more lbs WLL, Need X more tie-down(s))
Defective warning banner: '1 defective tie-down(s) excluded from calculation'
WLL Breakdown section: navy blue bars for direct, gold bars for indirect, red bars for defective
WLL Breakdown legend: Direct (100% WLL), Indirect (50% WLL), Defective (0 lbs)
Reset button clears all state (weight, length, tie-downs)
Back button navigates to home page (/)
Reference section toggles open/close with regulation content (393.106, 393.104)
Remove button deletes individual tie-downs
Math verification 1: 30000 lbs, 20ft, 3x 1" Ratchet Straps = 9900 WLL (66%) = NOT COMPLIANT
Math verification 2: 30000 lbs, 3/8" chain + 1/2" chain + 4" strap all direct = 23300 WLL (155%) = COMPLIANT
Math verification 3: Same with 3/8" chain indirect = 20000 WLL (133%) = still COMPLIANT
Math verification 4: With 1/2" chain defective = 8700 WLL (58%), count 2/3 = NOT COMPLIANT
Mobile viewport (390x844): All sections visible and properly laid out
NSP theme colors: Navy blue (#002855) header and direct bars, Gold (#D4AF37) indirect bars
seed_data_creation: None - calculator is stateless client-side only
context_for_next_testing_agent: Tie-Down Calculator at /calculator is fully functional. All math calculations verified correct per FMCSA 49 CFR 393 regulations. Key formulas: Required WLL = 50% of cargo weight, Direct = 100% WLL, Indirect = 50% WLL, Defective = 0 lbs. Min tie-downs: <5ft & <1100lbs = 1, 5-10ft = 2, >10ft = 2 + ceil((length-10)/10). All UI elements have data-testid attributes for testing.
